title_en: Chapter 22: How the Revelation used to descend upon the Prophet
title_ar: الفصل الثاني والعشرون: كيفية نزول الوحي على النبي ﷺ
title_id: Bab 22: Cara Wahyu Turun kepada Nabi ﷺ
content_id:
Telah disebutkan sebelumnya bagaimana Jibril 'alaihis salam membawa Wahyu kepada Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam untuk pertama kalinya (dari hadis Aisyah) dan untuk kedua kalinya (dari hadis Jabir). Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha meriwayatkan: Al-Harits bin Hisyam radhiyallahu 'anhu bertanya kepada Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam: 'Bagaimana wahyu ilahi datang kepadamu?' Beliau menjawab: 'Dengan semua cara ini: Malaikat terkadang datang kepadaku dengan suara yang menyerupai bunyi lonceng berdering, dan ketika keadaan itu berlalu, aku mengingat apa yang dikatakan Malaikat, dan jenis Wahyu ini adalah yang paling berat bagiku; dan terkadang Malaikat datang kepadaku dalam wujud seorang pria dan berbicara kepadaku, dan aku memahami dan mengingat apa yang ia katakan.' Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha menambahkan: 'Sungguh aku melihat Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam menerima Wahyu pada hari yang sangat dingin dan aku melihat keringat menetes dari dahinya (setelah Wahyu selesai).'

Dalam hadis panjang tentang peristiwa Ifk (fitnah), Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha berkata: 'Demi Allah, Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam belum berdiri dan belum ada seorang pun yang meninggalkan rumah sebelum Wahyu turun kepada beliau. Maka beliau pun ditimpa keadaan yang biasa menimpanya saat menerima Wahyu. Beliau berkeringat begitu banyak sehingga tetes-tetes keringat mengalir seperti mutiara, meskipun itu adalah hari yang dingin, dan itu karena beratnya pernyataan yang diturunkan kepadanya.' Dalam Shahih Muslim diriwayatkan dari 'Ubadah bin Shamit radhiyallahu 'anhu: 'Ketika Wahyu turun kepada Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, beliau merasakan beban karenanya dan warna wajahnya berubah.' Diriwayatkan dari Zaid bin Tsabit radhiyallahu 'anhu bahwa Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam mendiktekan kepadanya ayat: (Tidaklah sama antara orang-orang mukmin yang duduk (yang tidak turut berperang) dengan orang-orang yang berjihad di jalan Allah...) [Surah an-Nisa' (4): 95]. Zaid menambahkan: 'Ibnu Ummi Maktum datang ketika Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam sedang mendiktekan kepadaku dan berkata: Wahai Rasulullah! Demi Allah, seandainya aku mampu berperang (di jalan Allah), pastilah aku akan berperang,' — ia adalah seorang yang buta. Maka Allah mewahyukan kepada Nabi-Nya sementara pahanya berada di atas pahaku, dan pahanya menjadi begitu berat sehingga aku khawatir pahaku akan patah. Kemudian keadaan itu berlalu dan Allah mewahyukan: (kecuali orang-orang yang uzur).'

Ya'la radhiyallahu 'anhu melihat Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam ketika beliau sedang menerima Wahyu, dan ia melihat wajah Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam memerah dan beliau bernapas keras. Aisyah radhiyallahu 'anha juga meriwayatkan: Saudah radhiyallahu 'anha (istri Nabi) keluar untuk memenuhi hajatnya setelah diwajibkan kepada semua wanita Muslim untuk menjaga hijab. Ia adalah wanita yang berbadan besar, dan semua orang yang mengenalnya sebelumnya bisa mengenalinya. Maka Umar bin al-Khattab melihatnya dan berkata: 'Wahai Saudah! Demi Allah, kamu tidak bisa bersembunyi dari kami, maka pikirkan cara agar kamu tidak dikenali saat keluar.' Saudah kembali sementara Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam sedang berada di rumahku menikmati makan malamnya dan sepotong tulang yang masih ada dagingnya ada di tangannya. Ia masuk dan berkata: Wahai Rasulullah! Aku keluar untuk memenuhi hajatku dan Umar berkata kepadaku begini dan begitu. Kemudian Allah mengilhami beliau (Nabi), dan ketika keadaan ilham itu berlalu sementara tulang itu masih ada di tangannya karena belum diletakkan, beliau bersabda kepada Saudah: 'Kamu (para wanita) diizinkan untuk keluar demi keperluan kalian.' Ibn Katsir berkata: Dari sini dipahami bahwa bahkan ketika menerima Wahyu beliau tidak sepenuhnya kehilangan kesadarannya, dan buktinya adalah bahwa saat makan malam (beliau menerima Wahyu) namun tulang itu tidak jatuh dari tangannya.

Asma binti Yazid radhiyallahu 'anha berkata: 'Aku sedang memegang tali kekang unta al-'Adhba milik Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam ketika Surah al-Maidah diturunkan kepadanya secara lengkap, dan karena beratnya, hampir saja tulang-tulang unta betina itu patah.' Abdullah bin Amr radhiyallahu 'anhuma juga berkata: 'Surah al-Maidah diturunkan kepada Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam saat beliau sedang berkendaraan (di atas untanya). Dan (karena beratnya) unta itu tidak sanggup menanggung bebannya dan pun berlutut.' Imam Ahmad juga meriwayatkan tentang diturunkannya Surah al-Fath kepada Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam saat beliau pulang dari al-Hudaibiyah, dalam keadaan berkendaraan di atas untanya. Jadi terkadang keadaannya seperti itu (unta berlutut karena beratnya) dan terkadang tidak, tergantung situasinya. Dengan cara-cara itulah Wahyu turun kepada Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam — sebuah proses agung yang membawa kepada manusia petunjuk dari Langit.

Ringkasan cara-cara Wahyu turun kepada Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam: Pertama, melalui mimpi yang benar — ini adalah tahap awal Wahyu. Kedua, melalui ilham di dalam hati, sebagaimana sabda beliau: 'Ruhul Qudus (Jibril) telah mengilhamkan ke dalam hatiku bahwa tidak ada seorang pun yang akan mati sebelum sempurna rezekinya.' Ketiga, seperti bunyi lonceng — ini yang paling berat bagi beliau, namun setelah berlalu beliau mengingat sepenuhnya apa yang diilhamkan. Keempat, Malaikat datang dalam wujud seorang pria dan berbicara kepada beliau. Kelima, Jibril 'alaihis salam datang kepada beliau dalam wujud aslinya, sebagaimana disebutkan dalam Surah an-Najm. Keenam, Allah berfirman langsung kepada beliau sebagaimana yang terjadi pada Malam Isra' dan juga kepada Musa 'alaihis salam. Semua cara ini adalah cara-cara yang sah dan terbukti dalam sirah kenabian yang mulia.
content_en:
ﷺ It was already mentioned earlier how Jibreel عليه السالم brought the Revelation to the Prophet ﷺ the first time (from the hadith of `Aaishah) and the second time (from the hadith of Jaaber). Narrated `Aaishah رضي اهلل عنها: Al-Haarith Ibn Hishaam رضي اهلل عنه asked the Prophet ﷺ: “How does the divine inspiration come to you?” He ﷺ replied: ❝In all these ways: The Angel sometimes comes to me with a voice which resembles the sound of a ringing bell, and when this state abandons me, I remember what the Angel has said, and this type of Divine Inspiration is the hardest on me; and sometimes the Angel comes to me in the form of a man and talks to me, and I understand and remember what he says.❞ `Aaishah رضي اهلل عنها added: “Verily I saw the Prophet ﷺ being inspired Divinely on a very cold day and noticed the sweat dripping from his forehead (as the Inspiration was over).” 151 And in the long hadith of Ifk (slandering), `Aaishah رضي اهلل عنها said: “By Allaah, Allaah’s Messenger ﷺ had not got up and nobody had left the house before the Divine Inspiration came to Allaah’s Messenger ﷺ. So, there overtook him the same state which used to overtake him, (when he used to have, on being inspired divinely). He was sweating so much so that the drops of the sweat were dropping like pearls though it was a (cold) winter day, and that was because of the heaviness of the Statement which was revealed to him.” 152 And in sahih Muslim, it is reported from `Ubaadah Ibn Saamit رضي اهلل عنه: “When Wahee (inspiration) descended upon Allaah’s Messenger ﷺ, he felt a burden on that account and the color of his face underwent a change.” 153 151 sahih al-Bukhaaree (2, 3215) and sahih Muslim (2333-87) 152 sahih al-Bukhaaree (2661, 4141, 4750) and sahih Muslim (2770-56) 153 sahih Muslim (2334-88) [TN: `Ubaadah Ibn Saamit رضي اهلل عنه reported: ❝When Wahee descended upon Allaah’s Messenger ﷺ, he lowered his head, and so his Companions too would lower their heads, and when (this state) was over, he raised his head.❞ [sahih Muslim (2335-89)]

It is reported from Zaid Ibn Thaabit رضي اهلل عنه that the Prophet ﷺ dictated to him (the following Aayah): ﴿ َّّل ي س ت و ي ال ق اع د ون م ن ال م ؤ م ن ني﴾ {Not equal are those of the believers who sit (at home)…} And… ﴿ و ال م ج اه د ون ف س ب يل اللَّـه﴾ {…and those who strive hard and fight in the Cause of Allaah…} [Surah al-Nisaa’ (4): 95] ❞ Zaid added: ❝Ibn Umm Maktoom came while the Prophet ﷺ was dictating to me and said: ‘O Allaah’s Messenger! By Allaah, if I had the power to fight (in Allaah’s Cause), I would’, and he was a blind man. So Allaah revealed to his Messenger while his thigh was on my thigh, and his thigh became so heavy that I was afraid it might fracture my thigh. Then that state of the Prophet ﷺ passed and Allaah revealed: ﴿ غ يـ ر أ ول الضَّر ر﴾ {Except those who are disabled (by injury or are blind or lame etc)}❞ 154 And in the Saheehain, Ya`la رضي اهلل عنه saw the Prophet ﷺ when he was receiving the Revelation, and he saw that the face of Allaah’s Messenger ﷺ was red and he breathing heavily (like someone snoring). 155 In a long hadith, Abu Hurairah رضي اهلل عنه said: ❝(At this moment) revelation came to the Prophet ﷺ and when he was going to receive the Revelation, we understood it, and when he was (actually) receiving it, none of us would dare raise his eyes to the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ until the revelation came to an end.❞ [sahih Muslim (1780-84)]] 154 sahih al-Bukhaaree (2832, 4592) 155 sahih al-Bukhaaree (1536, 4329, 4985) and sahih Muslim (1180-8)

And in the Saheehain, `Aaishah رضي اهلل عنها said: Saudah رضي اهلل عنها (the wife of the Prophet) went out to answer the call of nature after it was made obligatory (for all the Muslims ladies) to observe the veil. She was a bulky lady, and everybody who knew her before could recognize her. So `Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab saw her and said: “O Saudah! By Allaah, you cannot hide from us, so think of a way by which you should not be recognized on going out.” Saudah returned while Allaah’s Messenger ﷺ was in my house taking his supper and a bone covered with meat was in his hand. She entered and said: “O Allaah’s Messenger ﷺ! I went out to answer the call of nature and `Umar said to me such-andsuch.” Then Allaah inspired him (the Prophet) and when the state of inspiration was over and the bone was still in his hand as he had not put in down, he said (to Saudah): ❝You (women) have been allowed to go out for your needs.❞ 156 (Ibn Katheer said): From this, it is understood that even while receiving Inspiration he did not completely lose his senses, and the evidence is that while he was sitting for dinner (he received the Inspiration), yet the bone (covered with meat) did not fall from his hand. صلوات اهلل وسالمه دائما عليه Abu Ya`laa reported that al-Faltaan Ibn `Aasim رضي اهلل عنه said: ❝While we were with the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ, he received Divine Inspiration. And when he used to receive Inspiration, he would stare continuously, his eyes would be wide open, and his hearing and heart would be freed for the (Inspiration) which he was receiving from Allaah عز وجل, and it was from this that we recognized it (that he was being Divinely Inspired).❞ 157 Imaam Ahmad and Abu Nu`aim reported that Asmaa’ Bint Yazeed رضي اهلل عنها said: ❝I was holding reins of Al-`Adhbaa’, the she-camel of the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ when 156 sahih al-Bukhaaree (4795, 5237) and sahih Muslim (2170-17) 157 Musnad Abu Ya`laa (1583) and al-Tabaraanee in al-Kabeer (856). (Shaikh al-Albaanee said): Al-Haithmaee said: “It was reported by Abu Ya`laa, al-Bazzaar, and al-Tabaraanee, and the narrators of Abu Ya`laa are all reliable.” It was also reported in Musannaf Ibn Abee Shaibah and sahih Ibn Hibbaan (4712), and it is as he (al-Haithamee) had said. [TN: Shaikh al-Albaanee graded is as “sahih” in “al-Ta`leeqaat al-Hassaan” (4692)]]

Surah al-Maaidah was revealed to him in full, and because of its weight, it almost broke the limbs of the she-camel.❞ 158 Imaam Ahmad also reported `Abdullaah Ibn `Amr رضي اهلل عنهما said: ❝Surah al-Maaidah was revealed to the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ while he was mounted (on his camel). And (because of its heaviness) it could not bear the weight and it kneeled down.❞ 159 (Ibn Katheer said): And it is reported in the Saheehain regarding the revelation of Surah al-Fath upon the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ while he was returning from al-Hudaibiyh, while mounted on his camel. So sometimes it would be this way (the camel kneeling down because of the weight) and sometimes it would be the other way, depending on the situation. And Allaah knows best. 160 158 Musnad Ahmad (27575, 27592). (Shaikh al-Albaanee said): Its chain is “Hasan li ghairihee”, see the next narration. 159 Musnad Ahmad (6643). (Shaikh al-Albaanee said): Its chain is “Hasan li ghairihee”, due to the previous narration. 160 I (al-Albaanee) say: It is possible to say: Only because the heaviness of the Wahee is not mentioned in this narration does not mean that it did not happen. Rather, the possibility is that it did happen but the narrator for some reason did not report it, like the way he did not narrate the effect of burden (it had on the Prophet ﷺ), which were mentioned in the earlier narrations. Like the hadith of Ibn `Abbaas, which will be mentioned later, and also this hadith of Ibn Mas`ood رضي اهلل عنه. He said: ❝We returned with the Prophet from al-Hudaibiyah…(then he mentioned that the Prophet ﷺ and his Companions stopped at a place to spend the night, and when they woke up)…the she-camel of the Messenger of Allaah ﷺ had strayed away. He asked to search for it. I found it (as its) rein had got stuck in a tree, and I brought it to the Prophet ﷺ. He sat on it while he was happy. When the Divine Inspiration came upon him, he felt the burden on that account, and it was from this that we recognized it (that he was being Divinely Inspired).❞ Ibn Mas`ood added: ❝He was riding behind us alone.❞ He further said: ❝So when the Divine Inspiration came to him, he covered his head with his cloth, and he felt the burden because of it. So we knew that he was receiving the Revelation. Then he ﷺ came to us and informed us that (the following) was Revealed to him: ﴿إ نَّا فـ ت ح ن ا ل ك فـ ت حًا م ب ينًا﴾ {Verily, We have given you (O Muhammad) a manifest victory.} [Surah al-Fath (48): 1] [Musnad Ahmad (4421) and the chain of this narration is “Jayyid”] [TN: The summary of different ways the Prophet used to receive Revelation: Good Vision: A Good or True vision was the first stage of Divine revelation to be experienced by the Prophet ﷺ [sahih alBukhaaree (3)]. And Ibraaheem said: {"O my son! I have seen in a dream that I am slaughtering you…”} and the Prophet ﷺ said: ❝The vision of a believer is the forty-sixth part of Prophecy.❞ [Saheehain]

Inspiration: The Prophet ﷺ said: ❝The Holy Spirit (Jibreel) blew into my heart (revealed to me) that no one will die till he gets his sustenance (that was decreed for him)…❝ [al-Saheehah (2866) of Shaikh al-Albaanee] Like the ringing of a bell: The Prophet ﷺ said: ❝Sometimes it is (revealed) like the ringing of a bell, this form of Inspiration is the hardest of all and then this state passes off after I have grasped what is inspired…❞ In the form of a Man: The Prophet ﷺ said: ❝…Sometimes the Angel comes in the form of a man and talks to me and I grasp whatever he says.❞ [sahih al-Bukhaaree (2)]. Also the hadith Jibreel; Companions also saw him in this form. In the original form: Jibreel عليه السالم twice came to the Prophet with the inspiration in his original form as mentioned in Surah al-Najm (53). Direct Speech: Allaah directly spoke with the Prophet ﷺ as happened on the Night of Israa, and also with Moosaa عليه السالم.]
content_ar:
أخبر رسول الله ﷺ عن أنواع الوحي وكيفيته. فقد روت عائشة رضي الله عنها أن الحارث بن هشام سأل رسول الله ﷺ: «يا رسول الله، كيف يأتيك الوحي؟» فقال رسول الله ﷺ: «أحياناً يأتيني مثل صلصلة الجرس وهو أشدّه عليّ، فيُفصم عنّي وقد وعيتُ عنه ما قال. وأحياناً يتمثّل لي الملك رجلاً فيُكلّمني فأعي ما يقول.» قالت عائشة: ولقد رأيتُه يَنزل عليه الوحي في اليوم الشديد البرد فيُفصم عنه وإن جبينه ليتفصّد عرقاً.

وقد جمع العلماء أنواع الوحي في عدة أقسام: منها الرؤيا الصادقة في المنام، ومنها ما يُنفث في روعه ﷺ من غير رؤية أحد، ومنها صلصلة الجرس وهي أشدّ أنواع الوحي، ومنها تمثّل الملك في صورة رجل فيُكلّمه. وقد أشار ابن حجر وغيره إلى أنواع أخرى منها: تكليم الله إياه من وراء حجاب كما حدث في المعراج، وإرسال الملك رسولاً في غير رؤية له كما حدث في بدايات البعثة. وكلّ هذا يدل على أن الوحي مراتب ودرجات تتفاوت في قوتها وكيفيتها، والله أعلم بحقيقة ذلك.
